AsPath.description: AS path information traversed by the BGP route entry.

Numbers.description: AS path sequence. If the AS path type is Sequence, the returned data is the original ASN sequence, such as [65533]. If the AS path type is Set, it indicates that the AS path has been aggregated, and the returned data is the aggregated ASN sequence, such as [4,5].

Type.description: AS path type. Sequence: normal ordered sequence; Set: aggregated sequence.

description: Add a route entry to the VPN gateway.

AsPath.description: AS path information traversed by the BGP route entry.

CreationTime.description: Time when the VPN gateway route entry was created.

DestinationCidrBlock.description: Destination network segment of the VPN gateway route entry.

NextHopId.description: ID of the IPsec connection for the route's next hop. You can call DescribeVpnConnections to query the IPsec connection ID.

NextTunnelId.description: Next hop tunnel ID. This parameter is returned only for VPN gateways in dual-tunnel mode.

RouteType.description: Route type. Static: static route; BGP: BGP route; Cloud: VPC route in the cloud. Note: Route types synchronized from transit routers (TR) are not currently supported for display.

Status.description: Status of the VPN gateway route entry. Creating: being created; Deleting: being deleted; Pending: being configured; Available: preferred route in effect; Conflicted: not preferred or not in effect. Note: When route prefixes are the same, the route priority is Cloud > Static route > BGP route. Therefore, there may be multiple routes with the same prefix, some with status Available and others with status Conflicted.

UpdateTime.description: Time when the VPN gateway route entry was modified.

VpnGatewayId.description: ID of the VPN gateway for the route entry to be added.

VpnGatewayRouteId.description: ID of the VPN gateway route entry.
